feedback.text: Ladies and gentlemen, please take into account an increasing problem in the digital gaming market under the Digital Fairness Act: Publishers completely deactivate paid games, making them permanently unusable, without compensation or alternative use. One example is the Battleborn game of 2K Games. I bought it at the full price (60) at the release. Today, the game is no longer playable, as servers and gambling access have been deactivated, even in single player mode. My legally acquired title is de facto deleted. This practice undermines trust in digital purchases and puts consumers at a severe disadvantage. Clear rules are needed to ensure that digital products remain permanently usable after purchase. Thank you!
